decks.notes: Control the game by utilizing lands to disrupt your opponent's strategy and slowly build up your own resources. Use cards like Life from the Loam to constantly bring back lands from your graveyard and cards like Crop Rotation to search for key lands like Dark Depths or The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale. Use Urza's Saga to create powerful artifacts and protect your lands with cards like Wasteland and Maze of Ith. With proper timing and strategic use of your lands, you can outmaneuver your opponent and gain the upper hand in the game.

The deck focuses on utilizing low-cost spells and aggressive creatures to control the tempo of the game. By disrupting the opponent's strategy with counterspells and land destruction, while also deploying threats quickly, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent and close out the game swiftly. Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent are key creatures that can quickly become large threats, while Lightning Bolt and Unholy Heat provide removal and reach. The deck also contains card draw and filtering to ensure consistency and maximize efficiency in each turn.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with efficient creatures and removal spells. By playing aggressive creatures like Sand Scout, Amped Raptor, and Ocelot Pride alongside powerful cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls, this deck aims to deal damage early and often. Additionally, the deck includes disruption elements like Static Prison and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the goal of this deck is to apply pressure on the opponent and close out the game before they have a chance to stabilize.

The deck focuses on using the combo card Doomsday to set up a winning situation. It includes cards like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to filter through the deck and find key pieces. Cards like Lion's Eye Diamond and Lotus Petal allow for fast mana acceleration, while disruption cards like Daze, Force of Will, and Thoughtseize help to protect the combo. The deck also includes powerful creatures like Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf as alternate win conditions. The goal is to efficiently and quickly assemble the combo pieces to secure victory.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's hand and board with cards like Thoughtseize and Swords to Plowshares while quickly deploying a strong board presence with efficient creatures like Guide of Souls and Orcish Bowmasters. The deck also utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and synergistic cards like Ocelot Pride to put pressure on the opponent and close out the game quickly. Additionally, the deck has some controlling elements like Static Prison and Karakas to handle opposing threats and protect its own key creatures.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing lands to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building its own powerful board presence. It uses cards like Wasteland, Thespian's Stage, and Crop Rotation to disrupt the opponent's mana base and lock down their key cards. Life from the Loam and Exploration help to generate value by repeatedly playing lands from the graveyard, while cards like Dark Depths and Urza's Saga provide powerful win conditions. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through resource denial and efficient land interactions.

The deck focuses on maintaining tempo and aggressiveness by using cheap spells and creatures to disrupt the opponent while developing its own board presence. It heavily relies on efficient card draw and filtering through Ponder and Brainstorm to find the necessary threats and answers. Instant speed removal like Lightning Bolt and bounce spells like Boomerang are used to keep the opponent off balance, while counter spells like Force of Will and Daze help protect the board. The deck also utilizes land disruption with Wasteland to hinder the opponent's mana development.

This deck aims to aggressively control the game through efficient creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Delver of Secrets, while using countermagic and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The key strategy is to tempo out the opponent by applying pressure while keeping their threats in check with a mix of direct damage and card manipulation. By leveraging card advantage and a strong mana base, this deck can quickly close out games before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the synergy between artifacts and blue cards to create a fast-paced aggressive strategy. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Urza's Saga are key cards that allow for card advantage and quick mana ramping. Force of Will provides protection while Kappa Cannoneer and Patchwork Automaton offer efficient threats. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of artifact creatures, disruption spells, and fast mana acceleration provided by cards like Lotus Petal and Mox Opal. The deck aims to establish a dominant board presence early in the game to secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on using Aluren to cast creatures for free, enabling powerful combos and card advantage. It utilizes cards like Show and Tell, Omniscience, and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to quickly establish a dominant board state. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Veil of Summer to protect its combos. By effectively utilizing Aluren and its supporting cards, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory through its powerful synergies and resource manipulation.

The deck revolves around utilizing Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis in combination with Stitcher's Supplier to quickly fill up the graveyard and bring Hogaak onto the battlefield. Wight of the Reliquary and Grist, the Hunger Tide provide additional threats while cards like Thoughtseize and Snuff Out help disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base is built around utilizing both green and black mana sources efficiently, while also having access to utility lands like Bojuka Bog and Wasteland. The deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disruptive spells.

The main idea of this deck is to aggressively control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing a strong creature presence. By utilizing a mix of efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and disruptive hand disruption cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize, this deck seeks to maintain board control and pressure the opponent with powerful creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Haliya, Guided by Light. The deck also includes mana denial with Wasteland and Karakas to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Ultimately, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggression and disruption.

The deck focuses on using Show and Tell as the primary combo piece to cheat powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield. By combining powerful spells like Sneak Attack and Lotus Petal with the ability to play cards for free using Omniscience, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and efficiently. With a mix of counterspells like Force of Will and disruption like Flusterstorm, the deck can protect its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan, leading to explosive victories.

The deck is focused on quickly milling through the entire library to win the game with Thassa's Oracle or using Dread Return to bring back key creatures. It utilizes a combination of fast mana sources, self-mill creatures, and tutors to assemble the necessary pieces for the combo. The deck also includes disruptive elements such as Pact of Negation, Cabal Therapy, and Chancellor of the Annex to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The goal is to win the game in a single explosive turn before the opponent can respond effectively.

The main strategy of this deck is to control the game by utilizing creatures and spells that can generate mana, ramp into powerful threats such as Craterhoof Behemoth, and disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Collector Ouphe and Wasteland. The deck also has toolbox elements with cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Natural Order to search for specific answers or win conditions. By efficiently managing resources and applying pressure at the right times, this Cradle Control deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly getting out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier by casting Show and Tell or Sneak Attack. It protects itself with counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce while also using card draw and manipulation like Brainstorm and Ponder to find the combo pieces needed to win. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its powerful creatures before they have a chance to set up their own game plan, utilizing the combo potential of Show and Tell to quickly establish board dominance.
